event.summary: Accepted the unit after passing validation and instruction synchronization.

acceptance.command: Review the M1 consolidation record, exact source checkpoints, completed private device receipt summary, and current automated validation.
acceptance.proof: The implemented no-ADB monitoring, exact Quest owner boundary, private bounded Quest checkpoint, durable restart, canonical CLI/API/WPF projections, keyboard/UI Automation, 1,000-device fixture, lifecycle, damaged-input, and cleanup evidence remain intact and passing.

acceptance.command: Review the updated README, implementation plan, validation guide, and M1 consolidation record.
acceptance.proof: The current-settings keyboard pass and preliminary Narrator confirmation are described without becoming comprehensive conformance evidence; unrun manual accessibility configurations remain explicitly open.

acceptance.command: Verify Milestone 7 retains the complete operator-attended accessibility suite and each intervening WPF milestone retains automated keyboard and UI Automation regression.
acceptance.proof: Deferring the cumulative manual suite does not remove accessibility requirements or allow regressions to pass automated milestone gates.

acceptance.command: Run the repository Standard gate and inspect the closed feature lock and M1 support statements.
acceptance.proof: ADB, File Manager, LSL, media, relay, and command capabilities remain inactive and unclaimed.

acceptance.command: Run the portable workflow contract validator over the additive supersession event and current workspace projection.
acceptance.proof: The original active M1 unit remains immutable, the exact supersession event selects this corrective unit as the only current authority, and no second in-flight unit exists.

document.commit_policy: Commit one documentation-and-workflow correction after Quick, Standard, and portable workflow validation. Do not repeat device or comprehensive accessibility gates and do not mix the next implementation milestone into this checkpoint.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the functional M1 closeout, preserve the no-device correction boundary, and keep the cumulative accessibility suite at the release gate.
Distinguish completed M1 functionality from the deferred cumulative release accessibility suite.
Keep automated keyboard/UI Automation at each WPF milestone and route the complete operator-attended suite to Milestone 7.
Replace the obsolete immediate manual-accessibility next action with truthful closeout and successor routing.
The correction changes project validation timing but not repo-family routing or public/private ownership.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill before acceptance.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: The correction preserves evidence vocabulary, authority boundaries, and validation honesty.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill before acceptance.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: No device action is rerun; the completed private M1 device checkpoint remains the exact evidence boundary.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill before acceptance.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: The additive correction does not alter the landed source graph.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill before acceptance.

non_scope: Claiming that Narrator, Accessibility Insights, high contrast, large text, display scaling, or multi-monitor accessibility has passed.
Repeating the completed M1 Quest checkpoint or changing headset, package, listener, ADB, Wi-Fi ADB, accessibility, display, or global Windows state.
Changing M1 runtime, WPF, Hub, Manifold, Quest Agent, LSL, media, command, ADB, or File Manager implementation.
Activating Milestone 2, Milestone 3, or any optional feature before this corrective closeout is accepted.
Rewriting the immutable fleet-m1-local-no-adb-monitoring unit or its earlier event prefix.
document.objective: Close the implemented Milestone 1 functional baseline without misrepresenting comprehensive manual accessibility evidence: preserve the completed authenticated no-ADB monitoring, Quest checkpoint, canonical CLI/API/WPF parity, automated keyboard/UI Automation, scale, lifecycle, damaged-input, and cleanup evidence; record the current-settings presented keyboard pass and preliminary Narrator confirmation as informative evidence only; move the cumulative Narrator, Accessibility Insights, high-contrast, large-text, scaling, and multi-monitor suite to the existing Milestone 7 release gate; and leave optional ADB, File Manager, LSL, media, relay, and command capabilities inactive.
outputs: A truthful M1 consolidation record distinguishing functional acceptance from the cumulative release accessibility gate.
Updated first-hop status and implementation/validation routing.
An accepted additive corrective workflow transition that preserves the original M1 unit and event history.
